top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 105, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
81ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Tour of Oman Sportive, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of Oman Sportive's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of Oman Sportive. Train it until it feels boring.
8,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 105 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
8,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Tour of Oman Sportive's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 105 miles.
105 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Low-cadence force work for 8,500ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: February in Muscat, Oman can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour of Oman Sportive. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour of Oman Sportive’s 105 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour of Oman Sportive. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 105 miles of mixed terrain in Muscat, Oman.
